title: Issue 989 Map macro with MapService MCP surface

questions.prompt: What should the first implementation goal include?
questions.description: Issue #989 describes a large end-to-end feature. The recommended scope keeps one coherent vertical slice while preserving the architecture that matters most.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Implement the full issue acceptance criteria as one vertical slice, but sequence the work so backend data model, reserved namespaces, MapService, and macro rendering land before optional polish.

questions.prompt: Which parts must not be deferred from this goal?
questions.description: These are the pieces most likely to affect the architecture if postponed.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Do not defer reserved namespace rejection, the central map mutator funnel, principal-derived attribution, MapService reads/writes, the Map macro, Leaflet rendering, and tests for the service and rendered macro behavior.

questions.prompt: What can be deferred only if the implementation gets too large?
questions.description: The issue marks several capabilities as v1, so the recommended answer is conservative and treats these as fallback deferrals, not planned omissions.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Prefer to include all acceptance criteria. If needed, defer only non-contract polish such as Esri imagery, OpenTopoMap, Storybook breadth, or fit-to-bounds button polish after the core MapService contract, OSM rendering, and tests work.

questions.prompt: What verification standard should the executing agent meet before calling this done?
questions.description: The repo convention expects devbox scripts and user-facing behavior coverage.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Run focused Go and frontend tests while developing, then finish with devbox run go:test, devbox run fe:test, generated files checked in, and any relevant e2e or Storybook build checks if touched.

questions.prompt: What must stay out of scope?
questions.description: Issue #989 already names several exclusions. This question confirms no hidden work should be pulled in.
questions.recommendedAnswer: Keep interactive map editing, routing, directions, geocoding, GeoJSON import, marker clustering, server-side tile rendering, and external paid/keyed map services out of scope.
